commands.repair_reason: A narrow automated repair can fix the plugin-policy leak and add focused regression coverage; maintainer/API acceptance can happen after the patch is correct.; Needs attention: The diff introduces a concrete tool-policy boundary concern: group:core can select plugin-owned tools in plugin-only resolution paths.; Review finding: [P1] Keep core groups out of plugin allowlists — src/plugins/tools.ts:87 (sha=85f979d1679d593e0b75275ef0e7098c46854704)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a documented `group:core` shorthand for all built-in core tools, updates tool-policy docs/tests, and expands `group:*` entries in plugin tool allowlist normalization.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main lacks `group:core` and documents `group:openclaw` too broadly; the blocking PR edge is also source-reproducible because plugin-only callers can match expanded core tool names without an `existingToolNames` core set.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can fix the plugin-policy leak and add focused regression coverage; maintainer/API acceptance can happen after the patch is correct.
